shelfmark: UTO Sinai Arabic NF M 66, SPP No. 336

layer_record.label: Sinai Arabic NF M 66, Undertext (Greek majuscule)

note: Only a few letters are visible. The parchment fragment is too small to reconstruct the number of columns and lines, but it is clear that the folio was made up from stitching together multiple pieces of parchment

bib.citation: Fleischer, Heinrich Leberecht. "Über einen griechisch-arabischen Codex rescriptus der Leipziger Universitäts-Bibliothek." In Zeitschrift der deutschen morgenländischen Gesellschaft 1 (1847)
note: (repr. in ibid., Kleinere Schriften III [Leipzig, 1888], p. 379-388, n. xxii), with a plate

bib.citation: Fleischer, Heinrich Leberecht. “Beschreibung der von Prof. Dr. Tischendorf im J. 1853 aus dem Morgenlande zurückgebrachten christlich-arabischen Handschriften.” In Zeitschrift der deutschen morgenländischen Gesellschaft 8 (1854)
Tchernetska, Natalie. “Cambridge University Library Add. 1879.5 and Its Provenance." In Homer, Tragedy and Beyond: Essays in Honour of P. E. Easterling, ed. F. Budelmann, P. Michelakis. London, 2001

Van Esbroeck, Michel. “Le codex rescriptus Tischendorf 2 à Leipzig et Cyrille de Scythopolis en version arabe.” In Actes du deuxième Congrès international d’études arabes chrétiennes (Oosterhesselen, September 1984), ed. S. K. Samir (OCA 226). Rome, 1986
Rossetto, Giulia. "Fragments from the Orphic Rhapsodies? Hitherto Unknown Hexameters in the Palimpsest Sin. ar. NF 66." Zeitschrift für Papyrologie und Epigraphik 219 (2021): 34–60

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
